summary:
The zygomatic bone forms direct articular contacts only with the frontal, maxillary, and temporal bones via specific sutures; it has no direct articulation with the nasal bone due to an intervening structure or lack of shared bony junctions. This makes choice C correct based on established cranial anatomy principles involving inter-bone relationships at the orbital region. 

Note that this summary focuses solely on clinically significant facts related to skeletal joints within facial structures as requested by user instructions for brevity and relevance.
